input.background: Consider a standby system with two identical valves each with constant failure rate $\lambda=10^{-5}$ failures/hours. The probability $\mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{s}}$ that the switch $S$ will activate the standby valve is 0.995.
input.question: What is the reliability of the system at time $\mathrm{t}=8760 \mathrm{~h}$ (or one year)?

output.answer: Here $p_s=0.995$, from Eq. (4.48) the reliability that the system will survive one year is, $$\begin{aligned} R(8760) & =\left(1+p_s \lambda t\right) e^{-\lambda t} \\ & =\left(1+(0.995)\left(10^{-5}\right) 8760\right) e^{-\left(10^{-5}\right) 8760} \\ & =0.9960 \end{aligned}$$
